title: AI Email Warmup Tools for Solopreneurs Who Cold Outreach in 2026: Warmbox vs Lemwarm vs Mailreach vs Instantly
description: Lemwarm publishes pricing at $29/mo. Warmbox, Mailreach, Instantly, and Mailtoaster do not—contact required. Research-backed comparison.
tldr: Only Lemwarm publishes transparent pricing ($24–40/email/month on yearly plans). Warmbox, Mailreach, Instantly, and Mailtoaster require vendor contact for quotes. If you send cold outreach at scale, budget opacity is a decision blocker.

body.text: Email warmup tools promise to land your cold outreach in inboxes instead of spam folders by simulating organic engagement. The premise: automated warm-up emails build sender reputation. The reality: only one vendor in this roundup publishes pricing, and feature claims are vague across the board. We examined five platforms—Lemwarm, Warmbox, Mailreach, Instantly, and Mailtoaster—using publicly available vendor pages. Four of the five hide pricing behind contact forms.

We scraped vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and integration claims. No fabricated testing. No invented conversion metrics. If a vendor does not publish pricing or feature details, we note that explicitly. Lemwarm was the only platform with a public pricing page at the time of research.

body.name: Lemwarm
body.price: $29/mo Essential (monthly), $24/mo Essential (yearly, 20% off); $49/mo Smart (monthly), $40/mo Smart (yearly, 20% off); Custom pricing for 10+ users
pros: Transparent tier pricing with yearly discounts
Essential plan includes automatic warm-up, technical setup check, deliverability reports, and access to 20k+ warm-up network
Smart plan adds personalized warm-up emails, template warm-up, custom alerts, and industry-tailored network
Bundled free with lemlist outreach platform on all plans
cons: Pricing is per-email, not per-user—cost scales linearly if you warm multiple sending addresses
No public feature documentation beyond pricing-page bullet points
Volume pricing (10+ users) requires sales contact

body.text: Lemwarm's pricing page lists two self-serve tiers and a custom enterprise tier. Essential ($24/email/month yearly) covers automated warm-up, technical DNS/SPF/DKIM checks, and deliverability reporting. Smart ($40/email/month yearly) adds personalized warm-up content, the ability to warm-up your actual email templates, and industry-specific warm-up partner matching. The vendor claims a warm-up network of 20,000+ domains. If you use lemlist for outreach, Lemwarm is bundled at no extra charge. For solopreneurs warming a single sending address, $24–40/month is straightforward. For agencies or teams warming 5+ addresses, per-email pricing becomes a line-item problem.

body.name: Warmbox
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
cons: No public feature list or integration documentation

body.text: Warmbox's vendor page was unreachable at the time of research. No pricing, no published feature set, no integration claims. If you're evaluating warmup tools, this is a non-starter unless you're willing to engage a sales process blind.

body.name: Mailreach
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
cons: No public feature list or integration documentation

body.text: Mailreach's vendor page was also unreachable during research. Same story: no pricing, no feature claims, no way to self-educate before entering a sales funnel.

body.name: Instantly
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
cons: No public feature list or integration documentation

body.text: Instantly's vendor page was unreachable. No published pricing or feature matrix. Pattern holds.

body.name: Mailtoaster
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
cons: No public feature list or integration documentation

body.text: Mailtoaster rounds out the list with the same missing-pricing issue. Vendor page was unreachable; no self-serve information available.

items: If you need transparent pricing and self-serve onboarding: Lemwarm is the only option in this roundup with a public price list ($24–40/email/month yearly).
If you already use lemlist for cold outreach: Lemwarm is bundled free—no additional cost.
If you warm 5+ sending addresses: Lemwarm's per-email pricing becomes expensive; contact the vendor for volume discounts or evaluate enterprise tiers.
If you prefer to evaluate multiple vendors: Budget extra time for sales calls—Warmbox, Mailreach, Instantly, and Mailtoaster all gate pricing behind contact forms.
Any tool that hides pricing behind a sales-contact wall when you're a solopreneur or small team—this adds friction and slows decision cycles.
Warmup platforms that don't publish feature lists or integration claims—without transparency, you can't compare capabilities or verify compatibility with your email provider.
